tips: Book accommodation near the motorway for easy departure next morning
Countdown supermarkets are cheapest for groceries

days.title: Drive to Rotorua — Hobbiton Option

days.title: Wellington Day + Departure

Wellington Cable Car (views over the harbour)

tips: Te Papa is free and easily fills 3 hours

packing_tips: Rain jacket for everyone — NZ weather changes fast
Swimwear and towels (you'll swim multiple times)
Reusable water bottles (save $20+/day vs buying water)
Snack bag for the car — petrol station food is expensive
Sunscreen — NZ UV is intense even on cloudy days

faqs.q: Can we do this itinerary in a campervan?
faqs.a: Yes, and it works even better. You save on accommodation ($40–80/night vs $150–350) and can cook your own meals. Campervans also carry everything you need. See our campervan comparison.

faqs.q: Is this itinerary suitable for toddlers?
faqs.a: Yes — most activities on this route work for under-3s. Agrodome, Te Puia, Huka Falls, and Te Papa are all great for toddlers. The Huka Jet requires minimum 3 years/90cm.

faqs.q: What's the best time of year for this trip?
faqs.a: December–February (summer) is peak season — warm, long days, everything open. January is school holidays (very busy). November and March are sweet spots — shoulder prices, fewer crowds, still warm.

faqs.q: Should we do a campervan or rental car for the South Island?
faqs.a: Campervan, if budget allows. The South Island has world-class holiday parks and freedom camping spots. A 6-berth campervan for a family of 4 saves $600–1200 in accommodation over 10 days. Compare campervan prices above.

faqs.q: Is this route suitable for toddlers?
faqs.a: Yes with modifications. Toddlers will love the animals (seals at Kaikoura, Abel Tasman birds), the beaches, and the playgrounds. Skip the helicopter and jet boat if under 3. The driving days are long — break them with short walks and stops.

faqs.q: Can we drive this in winter?
faqs.a: With caution. The Haast Pass and Crown Range can be icy — check road conditions daily at nzta.govt.nz. The West Coast and Westland glaciers are actually spectacular in winter with fresh snow. Carry tyre chains if renting in June–August.

title: 3-Day Auckland Family Itinerary
subtitle: Auckland city highlights for families
tagline: Make the most of Auckland with kids — from the waterfront to volcanic peaks
best_for: Families arriving or departing via Auckland, short trips, city breaks
transport: Hire car or public transport (AT HOP card)

faqs.q: Is Auckland worth spending 3 days in with kids?
faqs.a: Yes, if you use the time well. Auckland itself has good family attractions (Kelly Tarlton's, Auckland Museum, Cornwall Park) but the city's best family experiences are the day trips — Waiheke Island, the West Coast beaches, and Matamata (Hobbiton). 3 days is the right amount of time to do Auckland properly without rushing.

faqs.q: Do you need a car in Auckland with kids?
faqs.a: Yes, for this itinerary. Public transport works well for central Auckland but you need a car to reach the West Coast beaches and West Auckland properly. Hire cars from Auckland Airport are competitive — book in advance.

faqs.q: What is the best Auckland area to stay in for families?
faqs.a: Newmarket or Remuera (close to Cornwall Park and Auckland Domain), or the Viaduct/Wynyard Quarter (waterfront). Avoid Karangahape Road/Eden Terrace for families. North Shore (Takapuna) is good if you want a beach suburb base.

affiliate_blocks.heading: Find Auckland Family Accommodation
affiliate_blocks.text: Holiday parks near Auckland (Manukau, Papakura) offer great value. Or choose a central hotel for easy access to attractions.

affiliate_blocks.heading: Book Activities in Advance
affiliate_blocks.text: Kelly Tarlton's, Auckland Museum special exhibitions, and the Waiheke Island ferry can be pre-booked to guarantee entry.

packing_tips: Rain jacket — Auckland gets afternoon rain showers year-round
Swimwear and towels for Piha or Muriwai beach day
Reef-safe sunscreen — West Coast surf beaches reflect UV strongly
AT HOP card for public transport (buy at airport or convenience stores)

title: 14-Day Full NZ Family Road Trip
subtitle: Auckland to Queenstown — North and South Islands
tagline: The complete NZ family experience — geothermal parks, alpine lakes, fiords, and farm shows
best_for: Families who want to see all of NZ in one trip, ages 5+
transport: Campervan (strongly recommended) or rental car + accommodation
highlights: Rotorua geothermal parks and farm shows

faqs.q: Is 14 days enough to see both NZ islands with kids?
faqs.a: 14 days is the minimum realistic time to combine North and South Island highlights without feeling rushed. You'll cover 2,800km+ of driving, so a campervan is strongly recommended — it eliminates the nightly accommodation scramble and significantly reduces costs. This itinerary is designed for families with kids aged 5+ who can handle 2-3 hours of driving between main stops.

faqs.q: Should we fly between islands or take the Interislander ferry?
faqs.a: The Interislander ferry is the right choice for families doing this itinerary with a vehicle. The 3-hour Cook Strait crossing is a genuine experience — the Marlborough Sounds at the South Island end are stunning. Flying between islands requires leaving your vehicle (or hiring another on the other side), adding cost and complexity. Ferry bookings: 3-4 months ahead for school holiday periods.

faqs.q: How much driving is in this itinerary per day?
faqs.a: Average 2-2.5 hours per day. The longest drives are Auckland to Rotorua (2.5h), Wellington to Picton ferry (1h to ferry terminal), and Christchurch to Queenstown (4.5h on Day 12 — broken into two days in this itinerary). No single day requires more than 3.5 hours of driving.

faqs.q: What is the total cost for a family of 4?
faqs.a: Budget NZ$9,000-16,000 for 14 nights depending on accommodation choice. A mid-range campervan ($220/day + $50-70/night holiday park) gives total accommodation costs of around $4,000 for 14 nights. A rental car + mid-range motels works out to $5,500-8,000 for the same period. Activities typically add $2,000-4,000. Food in a campervan self-catering: $1,200-1,800.

affiliate_blocks.heading: Compare Campervans for This Trip
affiliate_blocks.text: A 4-berth campervan is the best way to do this 14-day itinerary — saves $1,500-3,000 on accommodation and eliminates daily packing/unpacking.

affiliate_blocks.heading: Book the Interislander Ferry
affiliate_blocks.text: The Cook Strait ferry (Wellington to Picton, 3 hours) is essential for this itinerary. Book 3-4 months ahead for school holiday travel.

affiliate_blocks.heading: Travel Insurance — Essential for 2 Weeks
affiliate_blocks.text: A 14-day trip across both islands involves mountain roads, adventure activities, and significant investment. Cover-More is NZ's most-recommended family travel insurer.

Cuba Street for lunch (NZ's coolest street, great cafes)
Zealandia Wildlife Sanctuary (optional, $55/adult — kiwi, tuatara, rare NZ birds)
tips: Te Papa is genuinely world-class and free — do not skip it
Wellington is NZ's most walkable city — leave the campervan parked

days.title: Cook Strait Crossing
days.drive_from_prev: 20 mins Wellington to ferry terminal, then 3h ferry, then 30 mins to accommodation
activities: Morning: Interislander ferry departs Wellington (multiple sailings daily — check timetable)
3-hour crossing through Cook Strait — dramatic scenery as you enter the Marlborough Sounds
Arrive Picton: explore the main street and foreshore (small, charming)
Drive to accommodation in Picton or Marlborough Sounds area
Evening: fresh seafood at a Picton waterfront restaurant (Marlborough green-lipped mussels are unmissable)
days.accommodation: Picton holiday park or Marlborough Sounds accommodation
tips: Book the Interislander 3-4 months ahead for school holiday periods
The Marlborough Sounds approach from the sea is stunning — go to the deck for the last 45 minutes
If sea-sick prone: take tablets before boarding, not after feeling sick

days.title: Abel Tasman Water Taxi Day
activities: Drive to Marahau (Abel Tasman National Park gateway)
Abel Tasman water taxi — take the taxi in, swim and explore Bark Bay or Anchorage, taxi out (book ahead)
The clearest, most beautiful water in New Zealand
Evening: dinner in Nelson CBD
tips: Book the water taxi 2-3 weeks ahead in peak season
The Abel Tasman coastal water is exceptional for snorkelling — bring a mask
Bring lunch from Nelson — food options inside the park are limited

days.title: Over the Ranges to the West Coast
activities: Drive via Murchison and Buller Gorge (stunning river canyon scenery)
Pancake Rocks and Blowholes at Punakaiki (free, 30-min loop walk — spectacular when seas are up)
Walk Truman Track to beach (10 mins — hidden gem)
Afternoon: arrival at Greymouth or Hokitika area
tips: Pancake Rocks are best at high tide — check tide times and aim to arrive at high tide for blowhole activity
Sandflies are significant on the West Coast — DEET repellent is essential before any walk

days.title: Franz Josef Glacier — Helicopter or Valley Walk
activities: Option A: Glacier helicopter flight with snow landing ($300-450/adult, $200-300/child, weather dependent) — spectacular but expensive
Option B: Glacier valley walk (free, 2 hours return to terminal face viewpoint)
Afternoon: geothermal hot pools in Franz Josef village
Rainforest walks in the Fox/Franz Josef area
days.accommodation: Franz Josef village holiday park or motel
tips: Helicopter flights are weather-dependent — have a backup plan
The valley walk is excellent even without the helicopter — the glacier is genuinely impressive from the valley floor
Hot pools ($35/adult, $20/child) are a great alternative activity if glacier is weather-cancelled

days.title: Drive to Wanaka via Haast Pass
days.drive_from_prev: 3h Franz Josef to Wanaka via Haast
activities: Drive the Haast Pass (one of NZ's most spectacular mountain roads)
Stop at Thunder Creek Falls (5 min walk from road)
Blue Pools walk in Mount Aspiring National Park (1h return, brilliant blue water, swinging bridge)
Arrive Wanaka, walk the lakefront, visit the famous Wanaka Tree
Puzzling World (if time allows, $25/adult, $19/child)
days.accommodation: Wanaka holiday park or lakefront accommodation
tips: Blue Pools require a shuttle or walk from the car park — check current access (minor slips can close the track)
Haast Pass is one of NZ's best drives — do not rush it

days.title: Queenstown Adventure Day
activities: Skyline Queenstown Gondola and Luge (morning — 45km/h descent, allow 2-3 hours)
Shotover Jet (book online: $149/adult, $99/child — 85km/h through narrow canyon)
Queenstown waterfront and Steamer Wharf for lunch
Afternoon: Remarkables lake views drive or Arrowtown village (35 mins, beautiful historic gold rush town)
tips: Buy a Queenstown combo pass if doing multiple activities — saves 10-15%
Arrowtown is excellent and free to wander — the Chinese settlement and autumn trees are beautiful

days.title: Milford Sound Day Trip
activities: Early start: drive to Milford Sound (2h 45m, stunning road through Homer Tunnel)
Milford Sound 2-hour cruise (book the earliest departure to avoid tour bus crowds)
Real Journeys or Go Orange cruises — both excellent
Waterfalls cascade directly into the sound — spectacular even in rain
Drive back to Queenstown for late afternoon campervan return
days.accommodation: Return campervan / fly home from Queenstown
tips: Milford Sound is spectacular in rain — the waterfalls triple in size. Do not cancel if it rains.
Depart Queenstown by 7am to catch the early cruise and avoid the tour bus crowd
Book the cruise 2-4 weeks ahead — popular departures sell out

title: Bay of Islands & Northland Family Road Trip — 5 Days
subtitle: Cape Reinga, dolphin cruises, kauri forests, and NZ's best beaches in 5 days
tagline: The classic Northland loop — dolphins, sand dunes, ancient forests, and Cape Reinga
best_for: Families with kids 4+, beach lovers, first-time NZ visitors

affiliate_blocks.heading: Campervans for Northland
affiliate_blocks.text: A campervan is ideal for Northland — holiday parks are everywhere and it saves $100+/night on accommodation.

affiliate_blocks.heading: Book Bay of Islands Activities
affiliate_blocks.text: Dolphin cruises and Hole in the Rock tours book out in peak season — reserve ahead.

budget_breakdown.activities: $500–$700 (dolphin cruise, dune sandboarding, Cape Reinga tour)

days.title: Auckland to Paihia — The Bay
activities: Drive north via Wellsford and Whangarei
Stop at Whangarei Falls (15min walk, free) on the way through
Arrive Paihia — check in, walk the foreshore
Late afternoon swim at Paihia Beach
Waitangi Treaty Grounds are walkable from Paihia foreshore (visit Day 2)
tips: Paihia TOP 10 Holiday Park is the best family base — walk to everything
Book dolphin cruise for Day 2 morning from Paihia wharf
Fuel up in Whangarei — Bay of Islands prices are higher
days.accommodation: Paihia TOP 10 Holiday Park or Paihia Beach accommodation

days.title: Dolphin Cruise & Waitangi
activities: Dolphin Discoveries cruise — swim with dolphins or watch from boat (book ahead)
Hole in the Rock passage — dramatic sea arch at Cape Brett
Afternoon: Waitangi Treaty Grounds — NZ's founding document site, cultural performances
Evening: Paihia foreshore restaurants
tips: Dolphin Discoveries dolphin swim is $89/adult, $55/child — book online
Waitangi is $40/adult, kids free — allow 2 hours
Paihia waterfront cafes are good value for lunch

days.title: Kerikeri & Cape Reinga Road
activities: Kerikeri: Rainbow Falls walk (30min return, free, beautiful)
Kerikeri Mission Station — NZ's oldest surviving building (1822)
Drive north to Kaitaia — base for Cape Reinga next day
Option: Doubtless Bay detour for a swim at Coopers Beach
tips: Coopers Beach is one of Northland's best — worth the small detour
Kaitaia is not scenic — use it only as a fuel and sleep stop
Stock up on food in Kaitaia — no supermarkets north of here
days.accommodation: Kaitaia motel or continue to Ahipara holiday park

days.title: Cape Reinga & 90 Mile Beach
activities: Cape Reinga Lighthouse — walk to NZ's spiritual northern tip (30min return)
Watch the Tasman Sea and Pacific Ocean collide — extraordinary
Te Paki Sand Dunes — hire boards at the gate ($20) and surf down 100m dunes
Ninety Mile Beach — drive on beach with a 4WD (do NOT attempt in a regular rental car)
Return via inland road through Te Paki farms
tips: Sand dune boards are available at the car park for $20 — worth every cent for kids
Do NOT drive on Ninety Mile Beach in a rental car — hire car insurance is void on beaches
Take a organised Cape Reinga bus tour from Kaitaia if you want beach driving experience
days.accommodation: Ahipara Holiday Park or return to Kaitaia

days.title: Waipoua Forest & Drive South
activities: Waipoua Forest — Tane Mahuta, the world's largest living kauri tree (15min walk, free)
Te Matua Ngahere — the 'Father of the Forest' kauri (40min return walk)
Trounson Kauri Park — evening kiwi spotting tour option
Dargaville Museum — fascinating Northland and kauri history
Drive south via Dargaville to Auckland (3h)
tips: Tane Mahuta is unmissable — genuinely awe-inspiring, all ages
You must stay on the boardwalks — kauri dieback disease kills trees via foot contact
Allow an early start to reach Auckland by early evening
days.accommodation: Back in Auckland or continue south

packing_tips: Reef-safe sunscreen — Northland UV is extreme Nov-Apr
Sandfly repellent for bush walks and beach evenings
Good sandals and beach shoes — you'll use them every day
Boogie board if driving in summer — beaches are excellent
Cash for Te Paki sand dune boards and smaller cafes

faqs.q: Is the Bay of Islands trip doable with toddlers?
faqs.a: Yes — Paihia beach is calm and safe, the dolphin cruise is exciting for toddlers, and Waitangi has good open space. Cape Reinga is a long drive for a toddler but the sand dunes are worth it. A campervan gives flexibility for nap stops.

faqs.q: How far is Auckland to the Bay of Islands?
faqs.a: 245km (about 3 hours) via State Highway 1. Traffic out of Auckland can add 30-45 minutes Friday evenings. Most families stop at Whangarei Falls halfway.

faqs.q: Should I do a Cape Reinga bus tour or drive myself?
faqs.a: Drive yourself if you have a rental car or campervan — you have more flexibility. Take a bus tour if you want the 90 Mile Beach driving experience (insurance voids on personal vehicles). Tours from Kaitaia are around $60-70 per adult.

highlights: Swim with dolphins in the Bay of Islands
Cape Reinga sand dunes — surf 100m dunes
Tane Mahuta — the world's largest living kauri tree

title: Rotorua & Taupo Family Road Trip — 5 Days
subtitle: Geothermal wonders, Lake Taupo, and Hobbiton in the heart of the North Island
tagline: The best of NZ's volcanic interior — geysers, Maori culture, skydiving lakes, and Hobbiton
best_for: Families with kids 5+, culture & nature lovers, Tolkien fans
best_season: Year-round (winter is great — geothermal sites steaming)

affiliate_blocks.heading: Book Rotorua Activities Early
affiliate_blocks.text: Hobbiton and Te Puia sell out for peak dates weeks in advance. Book your core activities the moment you confirm dates.

affiliate_blocks.heading: Rotorua Accommodation
affiliate_blocks.text: Stay centrally in Rotorua for easy access to all attractions, or at a lakeside holiday park.

days.title: Arrive Rotorua — Wai-O-Tapu & Polynesian Spa
activities: Wai-O-Tapu Thermal Wonderland — Lady Knox Geyser at 10:15am daily (book this!)
Champagne Pool, Artist's Palette, and colourful hydrothermal pools
Check in and relax
Evening: Polynesian Spa pools (family pool, $25/child, $38/adult)
tips: Wai-O-Tapu: $45/adult, $18/child — buy online to save 10%
Lady Knox Geyser erupts at exactly 10:15am every day (artificially triggered with soap)
Rotorua smells of sulphur everywhere — warn the kids, it fades in 20 minutes
days.accommodation: Rotorua TOP 10 Holiday Park or Wylie Court Motor Lodge

days.title: Te Puia & Whakarewarewa Village
activities: Te Puia Geothermal Park — Pohutu Geyser (NZ's largest), kiwi house, Maori carving school
Cultural performance at Te Puia (included in entry)
Afternoon: Rotorua Museum and government gardens (architecturally stunning)
Evening: Mitai Maori Village or Tamaki Maori Village cultural dinner ($95-130/adult)
tips: Te Puia: $60/adult, $30/child — allow 3 hours
Pohutu Geyser erupts frequently — check signage at entrance for recent activity
Mitai is more family-friendly than Tamaki (earlier finish time)

days.title: Hobbiton Movie Set
activities: Hobbiton Movie Set tour — 2-hour guided tour of the Shire from Lord of the Rings
Green Dragon Inn — included beer/cider/ginger beer at end of tour
Afternoon: return to Rotorua or continue to Taupo
Option: Agrodome Farm Show in Rotorua (if returning) — sheep shearing, working dogs, $45/adult
tips: Hobbiton: $55/adult, $27/child, $167 family (2+2) — book at least 4-6 weeks ahead
The tour is 2 hours on foot — bring snacks for young kids
Matamata Visitor Centre runs the shuttle — no need to drive the farm roads yourself
days.accommodation: Taupo TOP 10 Holiday Park (move to Taupo tonight)

days.title: Lake Taupo & Huka Falls
days.drive_from_prev: 85km from Rotorua (1h) or 0km if moved last night
activities: Huka Falls — most visited natural site in NZ, 30min walk from car park (free)
Wairakei Terraces Geothermal Pools — silica terraces and hot pools ($35/adult, $20/child)
Lunch at Lake Taupo foreshore — great playground nearby
Taupo DeBretts Spa Resort pools — outdoor thermal family pools
Optional: jet boat on the Waikato River ($60-90/adult)
tips: Huka Falls is genuinely spectacular — the most water per second of any NZ waterfall
Taupo playground at the lakefront is excellent for toddlers — right on the beach
If kids are older: lake cruises to the Maori Rock Carvings ($45/adult)

days.title: Tongariro & Depart
activities: Whakapapa Village — visitor centre, views of Ruapehu and Ngauruhoe (Mt Doom)
Taranaki Falls walk (2hr return, family-friendly, waterfall through lava fields)
Silica Rapids walk (2.5hr, through geothermal landscape, manageable for kids 7+)
Drive south to Wellington or return north
tips: Taranaki Falls is suitable for kids 5+ — well-formed track, no technical sections
Tongariro Alpine Crossing is adult-only (full day, challenging) — not suitable for families with young kids
Weather changes fast on the mountain — pack rain jackets even in summer
days.accommodation: Depart — return journey or next destination

packing_tips: Old shoes you don't mind getting muddy — geothermal mud everywhere
Swimwear and towel for every day — geothermal pools at most stops
Sunscreen and hat for the lake day — Taupo UV is high
Layers for Tongariro — temperature drops fast above 1000m
Hobbiton booking confirmation on your phone

faqs.q: How many days in Rotorua is enough for families?
faqs.a: 2 full days covers the core sites (Wai-O-Tapu, Te Puia, evening cultural show). Add a third day for Hobbiton if that's a priority. 5 days including Taupo and Tongariro is the full circuit and well worth it.

faqs.q: Is Hobbiton worth the money for kids?
faqs.a: Yes for kids who know Lord of the Rings or The Hobbit (ages 8+). For younger kids it's still a beautiful farm walk with fun hobbit holes. The included drink at the Green Dragon at the end is a highlight for adults.

faqs.q: What is the best geothermal park in Rotorua?
faqs.a: Wai-O-Tapu is the most colourful and varied — the Artist's Palette and Champagne Pool are extraordinary. Te Puia has the best geyser (Pohutu) and includes Maori cultural content. For families, visit both if budget allows.

highlights: Lady Knox Geyser eruption at Wai-O-Tapu
Hobbiton Movie Set — walk through the Shire
Huka Falls and Taupo hot pools

title: Nelson to Queenstown — 9-Day South Island Family Road Trip
subtitle: The best of the top half of the South Island — Abel Tasman, West Coast, Glacier Country, and Queenstown
tagline: Wild beaches, glaciers, gold rush history, and NZ's adventure capital in 9 stunning days
best_for: Families with kids 5+, adventure seekers, South Island first-timers

affiliate_blocks.heading: Campervan for This Route
affiliate_blocks.text: A campervan is ideal for this itinerary — holiday parks are perfectly spaced and you save $800+ on accommodation over 9 nights.

affiliate_blocks.heading: Pre-Book Key Activities

budget_breakdown.accommodation: $900–$1,200 (campervan hire included in total, or $1,200+ for motels)

days.title: Arrive Nelson — Settle In
days.drive_from_prev: Start in Nelson (fly in or ferry from Wellington via Picton — 2h drive)
activities: Arrive Nelson — pick up campervan or rental
Nelson city centre: Centre of New Zealand walk (30min, panoramic views)
Tahunanui Beach playground and swimming
Nelson markets if Saturday morning
tips: Tahunanui Beach is one of NZ's best family beaches — safe swimming, huge playground
Nelson has excellent supermarkets — stock up before heading into remote areas
Pick up campervan early to maximise the day
days.accommodation: Nelson TOP 10 Holiday Park or Tahunanui Beach Motor Camp

days.title: Abel Tasman National Park
activities: Abel Tasman water taxi to Bark Bay or Anchorage Beach
Swim in the golden sand bays — clearest water in NZ
Short coastal track walks between bays (30-60min sections)
Sea kayak option: half-day family kayak tour from Marahau ($75-95/adult)
Return water taxi to Marahau late afternoon
tips: Book water taxis with Aquataxi or Abel Tasman Sea Shuttles — 3 weeks ahead in January
Anchorage is the most sheltered bay and best for kids
Bring your own snacks — no cafes in the park
The park track between bays is tidal — check tide times
days.accommodation: Marahau Beach Camp or Awaroa Lodge

days.title: Nelson Lakes or Drive to Westport
activities: Murchison stop — good swimming at Buller River confluence in summer
Buller Gorge drive — dramatic river canyon
Option: Buller Gorge Swingbridge and flying fox (kids love it, ~$25 each)
Arrive Westport — Cape Foulwind walkway and seal colony (2hr return, free)
tips: Buller Gorge is one of NZ's best drives — stop at Hawk's Crag viewpoint
Cape Foulwind seal colony has hundreds of fur seals — bring binoculars
Westport is a good fuel and grocery stop before the more remote West Coast

days.title: Punakaiki Pancake Rocks
activities: Punakaiki Pancake Rocks and Blowholes — allow 1 hour (free DoC site)
Truman Track — 20min return through primeval forest to secret beach
Dolomite Point lookout
Afternoon: drive south along the coast to Hokitika (1.5h)
Hokitika: greenstone carving shops, sunset swim at Hokitika Beach
tips: Check tide times for blowhole viewing — best at high tide
Truman Track is magical — huge ancient trees right to the beach edge
Hokitika has good supermarkets — stock up before Franz Josef

days.title: Hokitika Gorge & Franz Josef
activities: Hokitika Gorge — 25min drive inland, 25min return walk, extraordinary turquoise river (free)
Lake Ianthe swim stop (calm, free DoC lake, optional)
Arrive Franz Josef
Franz Josef Glacier valley walk — 3km return to glacier face viewing area (free)
Evening: hot pools at Franz Josef Hot Pools ($30/adult, $20/child)
tips: Hokitika Gorge: go early morning for best turquoise colour (light angle matters)
The glacier has retreated significantly but the valley walk is still dramatic
Franz Josef village is small — dinner options limited, book ahead or self-cater

days.title: Fox Glacier & Haast Pass
days.drive_from_prev: 175km Franz Josef to Wanaka via Haast (3h)
activities: Fox Glacier walk — shorter than Franz Josef but stunning mountain reflections at Lake Matheson
Lake Matheson reflection walk (45min return, best light before 9am or near sunset)
Haast Pass drive — World Heritage wilderness, stunning waterfalls
Thunder Creek Falls (10min return walk off highway)
Arrive Wanaka — evening lakefront
tips: Lake Matheson early morning is one of NZ's iconic shots — Mt Cook reflection
The Haast Pass road is slow but spectacular — allow extra time
Wanaka is beautiful for an evening stroll — the lake is the playground
days.accommodation: Wanaka TOP 10 Holiday Park or Glendhu Bay Motor Camp

days.title: Wanaka Day
activities: Puzzle World — optical illusions and gravity-defying rooms, kids go wild ($24/adult, $18/child)
Wanaka lake and town beach swimming
Roy's Peak walk (only for fit families with kids 10+ — 5hr return, extraordinary views)
Rippon Vineyard and lawn for younger kids
That Wanaka Tree — the famous willow in the lake, 10min walk from town
tips: Puzzle World is genuinely excellent for ages 5-15 — allow 2-3 hours
Wanaka Beach is calm and safe for toddlers in summer
Glendhu Bay Camp (20min from town) is the most beautiful holiday park in NZ
days.accommodation: Wanaka TOP 10 Holiday Park or Glendhu Bay Motor Camp

days.title: Drive to Queenstown
days.drive_from_prev: 70km from Wanaka (1h) via Crown Range or SH6
activities: Crown Range Road — NZ's highest sealed road, extraordinary views (summer only)
Arrive Queenstown — cable car to Skyline for lunch views
Skyline Luge — 3 runs each for the family ($30-50/person)
Queenstown Gardens playground and lakefront
Evening: Steamer Wharf restaurants
tips: Crown Range is stunning but take it slow — sharp switchbacks with a campervan
Skyline Luge is the best family activity in Queenstown — book online to save 15%
Queenstown parking is difficult in peak season — use the waterfront parking buildings
days.accommodation: Queenstown TOP 10 Holiday Park (out of town but great facilities)

days.title: Queenstown — Last Full Day
activities: TSS Earnslaw steamship cruise on Lake Wakatipu ($62/adult, $31/child)
Walter Peak High Country Farm — farm tour and lunch (add-on to Earnslaw, ~$80/adult)
Arrowtown — 25min drive, gold rush village, Arrow River gold panning ($8/person)
Queenstown ice rink or mini golf afternoon
Farewell dinner on the waterfront
tips: Arrowtown is excellent for kids — gold panning in the river, historic buildings, great ice cream
TSS Earnslaw + Walter Peak is a highlight — book the morning departure for best light
Depart Queenstown via Christchurch (4h) or fly out direct

packing_tips: Warm layer even in summer — West Coast and Queenstown evenings are cool
Waterproofs for the West Coast — rain likely at some point
Sandfly repellent — essential at Haast, Glacier Country, and Abel Tasman
Reef-safe sunscreen for Abel Tasman beach days
Physical NZ map or downloaded offline — cell coverage patchy on Haast Pass

faqs.q: Is this itinerary doable in a regular rental car?
faqs.a: Yes — all roads on this route are sealed and suitable for 2WD vehicles. A campervan is preferred for flexibility but a rental car with motel/holiday park bookings works well. Budget an extra $200-300/night for accommodation vs campervan.

faqs.q: When is the best time to do this route?
faqs.a: December to February is ideal. Abel Tasman is best in January-February for warmest water. The West Coast is rainiest in winter. November is excellent if you want fewer crowds — school holidays haven't started yet.

faqs.q: How do we get from Wellington to Nelson to start?
faqs.a: Two options: (1) Interislander or Bluebridge Cook Strait ferry Wellington to Picton (3.5h, $90-150 per person), then 115km drive to Nelson (1.5h). Or (2) fly Wellington to Nelson direct (45min, ~$120/person). The ferry is spectacular with kids — highly recommended.

highlights: Abel Tasman golden sand beaches by water taxi
Queenstown Skyline Luge and lake views

faqs.q: Is 10 days enough to see the North Island?
faqs.a: 10 days covers the highlights well — Bay of Islands, Waitomo, Rotorua, Hobbiton, Taupo, and Wellington. You won't see everything (Coromandel, Hawke's Bay, East Cape are left for another trip), but this is a genuinely satisfying first North Island visit.

faqs.q: Is this itinerary too much driving for kids?
faqs.a: Most days are under 3 hours driving. Days 3 and 5 are the longest at 3 hours. Build in stops (Whangarei Falls, Tane Mahuta) to break them up. A campervan helps — kids can move around, play games, or sleep during drive time.

faqs.q: Can we do this in reverse (Wellington to Auckland)?
faqs.a: Yes — the route works equally well in reverse. Starting in Wellington and ending in Auckland gives you better morning light for Wai-O-Tapu and Tane Mahuta, and means you don't have to be at the Auckland airport on the first day.

highlights: Hobbiton Movie Set and Rotorua geothermal parks

faqs.q: How do we keep costs under $3,000 for a family of 4?
faqs.a: Three strategies: (1) Self-cater at least breakfast and dinner every day — saves $400-600 over 7 nights. (2) Use holiday parks instead of motels — saves $200-300 total. (3) Choose free activities (glacier walks, seal colonies, gorge walks) over paid ones where the experience is comparable.

faqs.q: Is a rental car cheaper than a campervan for this route?
faqs.a: A rental car plus holiday parks is typically $200-400 cheaper than a comparable campervan over 7 nights, especially in peak season. The campervan advantage is flexibility and eliminating check-in/check-out — worth it for longer trips of 10+ days.

faqs.q: Is the Kaikoura detour worth adding to a Christchurch-Queenstown trip?
faqs.a: Yes — Kaikoura is one of NZ's best wildlife experiences. The seal colony alone justifies the stop. Whale Watch is expensive but extraordinary. The coastal road between Christchurch and Kaikoura is scenic and breaks up what would otherwise be a long inland drive.

faqs.q: What is the best time of year to visit Northland?
faqs.a: November to April — warm enough for swimming, long daylight hours. January and February are peak (crowded and expensive). November and December are the sweet spot — summer conditions without the school holiday crowds.

faqs.q: Is the Waiheke Island detour worth it on a 5-day trip?

faqs.q: Should we base in Queenstown or Wanaka?
faqs.a: Both have great holiday parks. Queenstown has more activity variety and better restaurants for a first night. Wanaka is quieter, more scenic, and better value. Splitting 2 nights in Queenstown and 3 in Wanaka is ideal.

faqs.q: Is Wanaka or Queenstown better for families?
faqs.a: Queenstown has more organised activities (luge, steamship, jet boat) that kids love. Wanaka is more about lakes, nature, and Puzzle World. Both are excellent. Older kids (8+) usually prefer Queenstown; families with younger kids often prefer Wanaka's calmer pace.

faqs.q: Can we do skiing on this 5-day trip?
faqs.a: Yes in winter (June-September). Cardrona is the best family ski field — ski school from age 3, well-organised, 45min from Wanaka. Coronet Peak near Queenstown is steeper and suits older skiers. Budget an extra $150-200/person/day for lift passes and hire.
